tldr.text: Google's Gemma 4 12B lands as a laptop-ready, encoder-free multimodal model that runs on 16GB of RAM and handles audio, vision, and text natively.
India's Sarvam hit unicorn status with a $234M round led by HCLTech, while no-revenue AI startups are manufacturing billion-dollar valuations from thin air.
The Trump administration forced Anthropic to suspend its Mythos model over national security concerns, then partially lifted the ban — signaling a new era of government-gated AI releases.

categories.name: Tools & Products
categories.blurb: From multimodal models you can run on your laptop to a ChatGPT that rewrites itself mid-thought.

stories.title: Gemma 4 12B runs multimodal on your laptop
stories.summary: Google released Gemma 4 12B, a model that ditches separate audio and vision encoders entirely — both flow straight into the LLM backbone. It runs locally on 16GB of VRAM, supports native audio input, and is available under Apache 2.0 on Hugging Face and Kaggle. Performance reportedly approaches Google's larger 26B MoE model at less than half the memory footprint.

stories.title: GPT-5.5 Instant gets a readability overhaul
stories.summary: OpenAI updated GPT-5.5 Instant to produce more natural, better-paced responses with fewer bullet-heavy walls of text. As part of the update, the Canvas editing interface is being deprecated for this model tier — writing and code now live directly in chat via blocks. Paid users get a grace period on Canvas through legacy models before they're sunset.

stories.title: Google's May I/O recap: Gemini Omni, Googlebook, Universal Cart
stories.summary: Google's official May 2026 roundup confirms Gemini Omni can generate video from any combination of text, audio, image, or video input. The Googlebook — a Gemini-native laptop built with Acer, Asus, Dell, HP, and Lenovo — adds a Magic Pointer for contextual suggestions. Universal Cart lets users add items while browsing Search, YouTube, or Gmail, all from one checkout hub.

stories.title: Android's June drop adds book insights and safety tools
stories.summary: Google's June Android update brings Book Insights to Google Play Books, surfacing key ideas from select English titles — thousands of which are free. The drop also includes new personalization and safety features baked into the OS.

categories.name: Startups & Money
categories.blurb: Unicorns in India, robots in France, and a Forbes exposé on how pre-revenue startups conjure billion-dollar valuations.

stories.title: Sarvam is India's newest AI unicorn
stories.summary: Indian AI startup Sarvam raised $234 million at a $1.5 billion valuation, with HCLTech writing the lead $150M check. Bessemer, Khosla Ventures, and Peak XV also joined the Series B, which Sarvam aims to grow to $300M total. The company has already shipped open-source models at 30B and 105B parameters this year.

stories.title: No revenue, no problem: how AI startups inflate valuations
stories.summary: Forbes details how Ineffable Intelligence raised $1.1 billion at a $5.1 billion seed valuation despite having no product or revenue — its valuation jumped 70x in a matter of weeks between two tranches. Lightspeed, Index, DST Global, and Sequoia all participated at the elevated price. The UK's sovereign AI fund, backed by British taxpayers, also wrote a check.

stories.title: Baseten hits $13B valuation on $1.5B raise
stories.summary: AI infrastructure startup Baseten closed a $1.5 billion round led by Sands Capital and Wellington Management, pushing its valuation to $13 billion. Australian VC Blackbird made its largest-ever single investment in the round. Baseten provides the compute layer for businesses building and running AI tools.

stories.title: Genesis AI unveils non-humanoid robot with $105M in the bank
stories.summary: French robotics startup Genesis AI, backed by ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt, revealed its first general-purpose robot — pointedly not humanoid in design. The company has raised $105M, matching Mistral AI's record European seed round. Founded in early 2025, Genesis is betting that a non-humanoid form factor gives it an edge in a crowded field.

stories.title: General Intuition banks $320M to train AI on gaming content
stories.summary: General Intuition, which uses gaming data as its core training signal, raised $320 million in a Khosla-led Series A at a $2.3 billion valuation — and is already scouting a Series B. General Catalyst, Hedosophia, Bezos Expeditions, and Nico Rosberg also joined the cap table.

categories.name: The Bigger Picture
categories.blurb: Washington is now the gatekeeper for who can access frontier AI — and nobody voted on that.

stories.title: Trump pulls the plug on Anthropic's Mythos — then partially relents
stories.summary: The Trump administration ordered Anthropic to suspend customer access to its Mythos and Fable 5 models, citing national security concerns over Mythos's ability to exploit cybersecurity vulnerabilities at unprecedented speed. Anthropic complied but publicly disagreed, warning the move could set a dangerous regulatory precedent. By Friday, the government partially reversed course, allowing Mythos to be redeployed to a small group of vetted cyber defenders.

stories.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol also gated behind government approval
stories.summary: OpenAI announced that its new GPT-5.6 Sol model will only be accessible to customers pre-approved by the Trump administration, following the same framework applied to Anthropic. CEO Sam Altman negotiated directly with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick this week. A Democratic congresswoman called the arrangement lawless — "no law, no process, no oversight, just appointees deciding who's in and who's out."

stories.title: States pile on: Florida sues OpenAI, NY subpoenas follow
stories.summary: Florida's attorney general filed the first state civil lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, a sprawling 83-page complaint alleging the company prioritized profit over safety and engaged in false advertising. Separately, New York, joined by 41 other states, issued a sweeping subpoena demanding OpenAI documents on advertising, user engagement, data handling, and policies for minors and seniors.

stories.title: Court rules AI prompts are discoverable in litigation
stories.summary: A federal magistrate judge ordered a plaintiff to hand over the AI prompts its expert used to prepare a legal report, ruling they are part of the expert's methodology rather than protected drafting material. The case, Conservation Law Foundation v. Shell Oil, is one of the clearest signals yet that AI-assisted legal work leaves a discoverable paper trail.
